meta.purpose: Complete structured JSON prompt for building the /repair/blackmagic-hdmi page as a static Astro page. Every field contains real production copy — no placeholders. This is a niche/specialty repair page using a different layout than standard brand repair pages.
meta.templateNote: This page uses the NicheRepairTemplate layout, which differs from RepairLandingTemplate. It has: Hero → Proof/Video → Process Steps → Why Others Can't Help → Related Repairs → Why Choose Us → Mail-In Callout → Locations → FAQ. No services grid. No model list.

constraints.framework: Astro — static HTML only, zero client-side JavaScript
constraints.styling: Tailwind CSS with semantic design tokens from the project design system
constraints.icons: Inline SVG only — no icon library imports
constraints.images: Use with loading='lazy', explicit width/height, descriptive alt text
constraints.videos: YouTube embed via <iframe> with privacy-enhanced mode, lazy loading, and proper title attribute
constraints.interactivity: Use native <details>/<summary> for FAQ accordion — no JS. Table of contents requires minimal JS for scroll-spy (IntersectionObserver) — this is the ONLY permitted JS on the page.
constraints.seo: Single <h1>, canonical URL, JSON-LD (BreadcrumbList, FAQPage, Service), meta robots index/follow

seo.titleTag: BlackMagic Pocket 4K/6K HDMI Port Repair | BMPCC HDMI Fix
seo.metaDescription: BMPCC 4K or 6K HDMI not working? We repair BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera HDMI ports and controller IC chips. Mail-in service available Canada-wide.

mainEntity.name: Can you really fix a BlackMagic Pocket 4K HDMI port?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, we've successfully repaired BMPCC 4K and 6K cameras with HDMI port and controller IC failures. The HDMI components are similar to those we repair on gaming consoles daily, and our micro-soldering experience translates directly to BlackMagic camera repairs.

mainEntity.name: My BMPCC 4K has no HDMI output - is it the port or something else?

acceptedAnswer.text: HDMI output failure on the BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera can be caused by either the physical HDMI port or the HDMI controller IC chip on the motherboard. We perform board-level diagnosis to identify the exact failure point before recommending repair.

mainEntity.name: Do you repair the BlackMagic Pocket 6K as well?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, both the BMPCC 4K and 6K use similar HDMI port and controller IC components. Whether your BlackMagic Pocket 6K has intermittent HDMI or complete output failure, we can diagnose and repair both models.

mainEntity.name: How do you know if it's the HDMI port or the controller IC?

acceptedAnswer.text: We use board-level diagnostic equipment to test the HDMI signal path. This includes checking continuity on the port, testing the controller IC output, and verifying the communication between components. This dual diagnosis prevents wasted repairs.

mainEntity.name: Why can't I get my BMPCC HDMI fixed elsewhere?

acceptedAnswer.text: BlackMagic doesn't offer component-level repairs — they typically suggest replacement cameras. Most local repair shops lack the specialized micro-soldering equipment and experience needed for professional cinema camera board-level repair. Our console HDMI repair experience gives us the skills to tackle these jobs.

mainEntity.name: Do you accept mail-in BlackMagic camera repairs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, we regularly receive BMPCC 4K and 6K cameras from filmmakers and content creators across Canada. We provide guidance on proper packaging to ensure your camera arrives safely for HDMI port or controller IC repair.

mainEntity.name: How should I ship my BlackMagic camera for HDMI repair?

acceptedAnswer.text: Remove any accessories (lens, cage, handle, SSD). Use the original box if available, or pack securely with foam or bubble wrap in a sturdy box. We recommend insured shipping with tracking. Contact us before shipping and we'll provide detailed packaging instructions.

mainEntity.name: What if both the HDMI port and controller IC need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: If diagnosis reveals both the HDMI port and controller IC have failed, we can replace both components. We'll provide a quote for the full repair before proceeding, so you know exactly what's needed to restore HDMI output on your BlackMagic camera.

serviceSchema.name: BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera HDMI Repair
serviceSchema.description: Professional HDMI port and controller IC repair for BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera 4K and 6K. Board-level micro-soldering by experienced technicians.

visible_breadcrumbs.instructions: Render a visible breadcrumb nav above the hero. Use semantic <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'> with an <ol>. Style: small text, muted color, '/' separator between items. Last item is plain text (current page), others are links.

section_1_hero.instructions: Full-width hero with gradient background (from-background via-background to-primary/5). Centered text layout — no image in hero. Two badges above headline. Two CTA buttons: Call and Text. Decorative blurred circles in corners.

badges.label: Pro Video Equipment Specialist
badges.style: destructive — red-tinted background, red text, AlertTriangle icon before text

badges.label: Mail-In Service Available
badges.style: primary — primary-tinted background, primary text, Package icon before text

section_1_hero.headline: BlackMagic Pocket 4K/6K HDMI Repair
section_1_hero.heroDescription: Is your BMPCC 4K or 6K showing no HDMI output? We fix BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera HDMI ports and the controller IC chip when others can't. Based in Winnipeg, MB - mail-in repairs welcome from anywhere in Canada.
decorativeElements: Blurred circle: absolute -bottom-24 -right-24 h-64 w-64 rounded-full bg-primary/5 blur-3xl
Blurred circle: absolute -top-24 -left-24 h-64 w-64 rounded-full bg-primary/5 blur-3xl

section_2_proof_video.instructions: Two-column section on md+, stacked on mobile. Left column: YouTube embed in a rounded, bordered, shadowed container with 16:9 aspect ratio. Right column: heading, caption paragraph, and two CTA buttons (Call + Text). Background: muted/30 with top and bottom borders.
section_2_proof_video.youtubeVideoTitle: Watch Our BMPCC HDMI Repair Process

iframeAttributes.allow: accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share

section_2_proof_video.rightColumnCaption: Our professional micro-soldering workstation where we diagnose and repair BMPCC 4K and 6K HDMI port and controller IC failures. We use the same precision equipment for gaming console HDMI repairs.

fallbackImage.note: If youtubeVideoId is removed, show the proof image instead with a 'Repair Complete' badge
fallbackImage.alt: BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera HDMI port repair workstation

section_3_repair_process.instructions: Section heading centered, then a 2-column grid of 4 numbered step cards. Each card has a numbered circle (bg-primary text-primary-foreground), a bold title, and a description paragraph. Cards have rounded borders and subtle shadow.
section_3_repair_process.sectionSubheading: How we tackle repairs that others won't even attempt

steps.title: HDMI Diagnosis
steps.description: Board-level inspection to determine if the failure is the HDMI port itself or the HDMI controller IC chip on the motherboard. We test both failure points before recommending repair.

steps.title: Component Sourcing
steps.description: Locating compatible HDMI ports or controller ICs — often cross-referencing with similar components we already use for PS5, Xbox, and Switch HDMI repairs.

steps.title: Micro-Soldering Repair
steps.description: Precision removal and replacement of the failed HDMI port or controller IC using professional BGA rework equipment and proper thermal management.

steps.title: Signal & Output Testing
steps.description: Full HDMI output testing at multiple resolutions including 4K/6K to verify clean signal and proper handshake before returning your camera.

cardStyle.container: rounded-xl border border-border bg-card p-6 shadow-sm
cardStyle.numberCircle: flex h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-primary text-primary-foreground font-bold
cardStyle.title: text-lg font-semibold text-foreground

section_4_why_others_cant_help.instructions: Section with muted background and top/bottom borders. Centered heading, then a 3-column grid of problem cards. Each card has a destructive/red theme: red-tinted border, red-tinted background, icon in a red circle, bold title, description. This section builds urgency by showing the customer has limited options.
section_4_why_others_cant_help.sectionSubheading: Most repair shops turn away these devices — here's why we don't

problems.title: No Manufacturer Support
problems.description: BlackMagic doesn't offer HDMI port repair — a replacement camera is often the only 'official' option for BMPCC 4K or 6K HDMI failures.

problems.title: Repair Shops Decline
problems.description: Most electronics repair shops don't have experience with professional cinema cameras or the micro-soldering skills required for BMPCC HDMI repair.

problems.title: Two Possible Failure Points
problems.description: The HDMI issue could be the port OR the controller IC chip — misdiagnosis leads to wasted repairs. Most shops can't properly diagnose both.

cardStyle.iconCircle: flex h-12 w-12 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-destructive/10 text-destructive
cardStyle.title: text-lg font-semibold text-foreground

section_5_related_repairs.sectionSubheading: We fix HDMI ports on other devices too

section_9_faq.instructions: Render FAQs as a native <details>/<summary> accordion — no JavaScript. Must match seo.faqSchema exactly — same 8 questions and answers. Wrap in a section with centered heading.
section_9_faq.sectionHeading: BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera Repair FAQs
section_9_faq.sectionSubheading: Common questions about BlackMagic Pocket Cinema Camera repair at Mobile Tech Lab
section_9_faq.note: Copy FAQ items exactly from seo.faqSchema.mainEntity — do not generate new items. There are 8 FAQ items.

accordionStyle.summary: cursor-pointer py-4 text-left font-medium text-foreground hover:text-primary

section_10_sticky_cta.instructions: Fixed bottom bar visible only on mobile (md:hidden). Contains two equal-width buttons: Call and Text. Stays fixed at bottom of viewport with backdrop blur.
section_10_sticky_cta.style: fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 border-t border-border bg-background/95 p-4 backdrop-blur-sm md:hidden

section_11_table_of_contents.instructions: Floating TOC button in the bottom-right corner (bottom-24 on mobile to clear sticky CTA, bottom-8 on desktop). Clicking opens a small popover with section links. This is the ONLY component that requires JavaScript — use a minimal IntersectionObserver for scroll-spy highlighting, and a simple toggle for open/close. Keep the JS inline and under 30 lines.

triggerButton.style: h-12 w-12 rounded-full border border-border bg-card shadow-lg

shared_layout.instructions: Wrap the entire page in the shared site layout. The layout includes: sticky header with navigation, desktop CTA, footer with links, and the mobile sticky CTA bar.
shared_layout.header: Shared site header — use the same header component as all other pages
shared_layout.footer: Shared site footer — use the same footer component as all other pages
shared_layout.mainTag: Wrap all sections in <main class='flex-1 pb-20 md:pb-0'> — pb-20 on mobile to clear the sticky CTA bar

validation_checklist.instructions: Before returning the built Astro page, verify every item on this list is true
checks: faqSchema items match section_9_faq content exactly
section_8_locations data is identical to the provided constants — not paraphrased
YouTube embed uses privacy-enhanced mode or standard embed URL
All section IDs match the table_of_contents items
Sticky CTA bar is md:hidden only
No placeholder text — every string field has real content
Only one <h1> tag on the entire page
Output is valid HTML — no unclosed tags, no React/JSX syntax
Zero client-side JavaScript except the minimal TOC scroll-spy
